violations.description: General employee practices. — --Employee food and drinks were being stored on the cutting board of the front prep cooler.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. Food was being stored in dirty buckets in the walk in cooler.--Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of food were stored on the floor in the back walk in cooler.
Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Deli Express and Tielke sandwiches were not dated when removed from the freezer.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Food buckets in the walk in cooler need to be cleaned.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Three compartment sink was not maintained or operated in the approved three step method. Washing was being done in the middle sink, and not in the first sink. Needs to be wash, rinse, sanitize.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Black plastic food containers on the kitchen storage shelves were not covered, or inverted. Pizza and food containers under the front counter in the kitchen were not inverted.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. The hand sink in the kitchen had items stored in it. The other hand sink had a sanitize bucket stored in it.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Both paper towel dispensers for the kitchen hand sinks were out of paper towels.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. A can of linen fresh air freshener/deodorizer was stored on pizza pans on the storage shelves in the kitchen.
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Available, knowledgeable, certified manager (4 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
